content.full_text:
CHARLES E. BRAY, CPA

BUSINESS AND FINANCIAL CONSULTANT

2626 NICHOLSON STREET

SAN LEANDRO, CALIFORNIA 94577

(415) 357-7269

July 19, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Attention: Peggy Bernardi

Dear Peggy:

Enclosed is the letter and itinerary for the China trip which
Sheriff Charles Plummer has been invited.

Please advise if the trip can be paid for out of campaign
funds.

Since time is of the essence, please respond as soon as
possible.

Sincerely,

Karen Bass
Accountant

Karen: Reference our conversation this date.     Jane     7/5/90

ROBERT L. SMITH
71 FAIRLAWN DRIVE
BERKELEY, CALIFORNIA 94708
(415) 845-2214

[handwritten text - illegible]

June 20, 1990

Sheriff Charles C. Plummer
1225 Fallon Street
Oakland, CA  94612

Dear Sheriff Plummer:

The Chinese Bar Association has invited a North American delegation of professional and citizen leaders involved in the administration of juvenile and criminal justice to visit China this October. The project is being organized under the auspices of the Citizen Ambassador Program.  I have been asked to lead the delegation, and I am pleased to invite you to become a member of the team.

As you know, events in Tian'anmen Square in June 1989 caused a major setback in Chinese/American relations.  The epic of the hard-line stance of Chinese political leaders.  Western observers believe that normal relations can be restored by keeping the lines of communication open.  Many mainstream Chinese are shamed by the regrettable actions of their government and are urging the West not to abandon or isolate the Chinese people.  People-to-people exchanges are being encouraged again, since they played an important role during the 1970s in normalizing U.S. relations with China.  Therefore, the invitation from the Chinese Bar Association to reestablish professional contact to continue the dialogue on criminal justice matters is encouraging and is an opportunity we cannot afford to pass up.  I therefore hope you can participate in what will be an historic interchange.

As indicated in the enclosed Proposed Schedule of Activities, our delegation will convene in Beijing on October 9 and will visit Beijing, Shanghai, Hangzhou, and Hong Kong, returning home on October 23, 1990 (15 days).  The estimated expense per delegate or guest is U.S. $3175 (plus domestic and international airfare); this includes all professional activities, transportation within China, accommodations, all meals in China, most meals in Hong Kong, and cultural and sightseeing activities.  Expenses related to your participation may be deductible under Sections 162 and 274 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 as an ordinary and necessary business expense or as an educational expense.  Participants may consult with a tax advisor to determine whether tax deductibility applies.

The Citizen Ambassador Program will coordinate our travel and professional arrangements with our hosts, the Chinese Bar Association.  While in China, we will participate in a full range of briefings, meetings, and discussion sessions, covering adult and juvenile criminal justice from arrest, prosecution, and sentencing to treatment and correction.  Field visits are planned to courts and to police and correctional facilities; we will also observe Chinese-neighborhood mediation committees in action, and will meet with Ministry of Public Security

CJMC  EXECUTIVE AND MANAGEMENT CONSULTATION  I  CRIMINAL AND JUVENILE JUSTICE ADMINISTRATION

Page 2

officials. In addition, social encounters, sightseeing, and cultural activities are planned in each city.
Spouses and guests are also invited and are welcome to attend the professional meetings and social
functions.

On two past occasions, I have led criminal justice teams to China for the Citizen Ambassador
Program, each prior to June 1989. My involvement in these exchanges has provided some of the
more memorable personal and professional experiences of my career, which spans over 40 years in
juvenile and criminal justice. I urge you to seriously consider joining our delegation to share in
these experiences and to contribute in your own way to furthering Chinese/American relations.

If you plan to join the delegation, it is important that you call Malinda Sautter, Director of
Legal Projects for the Citizen Ambassador Program, or Julie Edwards, Project Coordinator, at
(509) 534-0430, as soon as possible, since our Chinese hosts are limiting our team to 30 members,
including spouses and guests. I have enclosed a registration form that I urge you to send to the
Citizen Ambassador Program office at your earliest convenience. Should you receive agency or
other external assistance that involves waiting for approvals, I suggest that you make a tentative
reservation.

We look forward to hearing from you.

Sincerely,

Robert L. Smith

RLS/je

Enclosures

cc:    Malinda Sautter
       Director, Legal Projects
       People to People Citizen Ambassador Program
       Dwight D. Eisenhower Building
       Spokane, Washington 99202
